steps.title: Identify the specific metric for measuring stress reduction
steps.content: The passage specifies that researchers “compared the percent change between baseline and peak heart rate” with “higher values indicating higher stress levels.” For social buffering to occur, a companion should reduce this stress metric.

steps.title: Match the measurement to the choice
steps.content: Choice C directly states that “the average percent change in heart rate was lower among C. helleri with a companion than among solitary C. helleri and C. helleri with a rope”—this is exactly what the researchers measured, and a lower percent change directly demonstrates that companions reduce stress response. Choices A and B report absolute heart rate values rather than percent change, while D contradicts social buffering by showing percent change is smaller when solitary. That matches choice C — C is correct.
